seo.title: AI Agent Error Handling Patterns in AG2 Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent error handling patterns in AG2 agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In AG2, AI Agent Error Handling Patterns manifest as uncontrolled conversation drift, factual inaccuracies in group consensus, unsafe code generation, and biased speaker selection in multi-agent debates. These issues arise from AG2's enhanced group chat features where agents lack robust termination conditions, fact-checking mechanisms, and safety guards. Without proper error boundaries, conversations spiral, producing unreliable or harmful outputs.

howItManifests.symptom: GroupChatManager continues rounds indefinitely despite irrelevant responses
howItManifests.impact: Wastes compute, produces irrelevant outputs, never reaches termination condition

howItManifests.scenario: Group chat consensus on incorrect facts
howItManifests.symptom: Agents reinforce each other's factual errors through repeated agreement
howItManifests.impact: Produces confidently wrong answers that appear authoritative

howItManifests.scenario: Code execution agents producing harmful outputs
howItManifests.symptom: CodeExecutorAgent generates dangerous system commands without sandboxing
howItManifests.impact: Potential security breaches, data corruption, or system damage

howItManifests.scenario: Speaker selection bias in multi-agent groups
howItManifests.symptom: SpeakerSelectionMethod favors dominant agents, silencing others
howItManifests.impact: Biased decisions, groupthink, and suboptimal solutions

detection.name: Conversation Drift Detector
detection.description: Monitors topic deviation using keyword overlap between initial prompt and recent messages
detection.code:
from ag2.groupchat import GroupChat
from collections import Counter

def detect_drift(initial_prompt: str, recent_messages: list[str], threshold: float = 0.3) -> bool:
    initial_words = set(initial_prompt.lower().split())
    recent_text = ' '.join([msg.content for msg in recent_messages[-5:]])
    recent_words = set(recent_text.lower().split())
    overlap = len(initial_words.intersection(recent_words)) / len(initial_words)
    return overlap < threshold

detection.name: Fact Consensus Validator
detection.description: Detects when multiple agents agree on potentially false factual claims
detection.code:
import re
from ag2.core.message import Message

def detect_false_consensus(messages: list[Message]) -> dict:
    facts = []
    for msg in messages:
        # Extract potential facts (simple heuristic)
        fact_pattern = r'(\b[A-Z][a-z]+ \d{4}\b|\bTrue\b|\bFalse\b)'
        facts.extend(re.findall(fact_pattern, msg.content))
    
    fact_votes = Counter(facts)
    suspicious = {f: count for f, count in fact_votes.items() if count > 2}
    return suspicious

detection.name: Speaker Dominance Monitor
detection.description: Tracks speaker selection imbalance in GroupChat
detection.code:
from collections import Counter
from ag2.groupchat import GroupChat

def detect_speaker_bias(chat_history: list) -> dict:
    speakers = [msg.sender for msg in chat_history if hasattr(msg, 'sender')]
    speaker_counts = Counter(speakers)
    total_msgs = len(speakers)
    dominance_ratio = max(speaker_counts.values()) / total_msgs if total_msgs > 0 else 0
    return {
        'dominance_ratio': dominance_ratio,
        'dominant_speaker': speaker_counts.most_common(1)[0][0] if speaker_counts else None
    }

fixes.name: Topic Guard Termination
fixes.description: Implement max_rounds and drift-based termination in GroupChatManager

fixes.name: Fact-Checking Interceptor
fixes.description: Add human-in-loop validation for high-confidence factual claims

fixes.name: Balanced Speaker Selection
fixes.description: Implement round-robin speaker selection to prevent dominance

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Error Handling Patterns Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with AG2 as a message hook, automatically monitoring all agent conversations for the four key error patterns. It runs the drift detection, consensus validation, speaker bias analysis, and code safety checks in real-time, flagging issues before they cascade. When problems are detected, Vex can automatically inject corrective agents, terminate spiraling conversations, or request human intervention.

The Vex SDK provides pre-built detectors for AG2's specific failure modes, with configurable thresholds and remediation actions. Integration requires just one line of code to register Vex as a callback on your GroupChatManager. Vex maintains conversation state across sessions and provides dashboards for analyzing error patterns across deployments.

Remediation is fully automated: Vex can replace biased speaker selection methods, inject fact-checking agents, enforce conversation TTLs, and sandbox code execution automatically based on detected risk levels.
vexAutomation.code:
from ag2.groupchat import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from vex_sdk import VexMonitor

# Install: pip install ag2 vex-sdk

vex = VexMonitor(
    detectors=[
        'conversation_drift',
        'false_consensus',
        'speaker_bias',
        'code_safety'
    ],
    auto_remediate=True,
    max_drift_threshold=0.3
)

# Single line integration
chat_manager = GroupChatManager(
    groupchat=GroupChat(agents=[agent1, agent2, agent3]),
    message_hooks=[vex.hook]  # Vex monitors all messages
)

# Vex auto-terminates on drift > 30%
# Vex injects fact-checker on consensus detection
# Vex enforces round-robin speaker selection
await chat_manager.run_chat(initial_message)

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex with `pip install ag2 vex-sdk` and add one line to eliminate AI agent error patterns forever.
